settings.title: Welcoming Sol to the Inkwell Chairs
settings.excerpt: Our newest resident brings a fine line and botanical style we have wanted in the studio for a while.
settings.body:
We are glad to introduce Sol, who joins Inkwell as our fourth resident artist this month.

Sol works in delicate fine line and botanical pieces, the kind of quiet, detailed work that rewards a close look. It fills a gap in what we could offer, and we have already seen the books filling up.

What Sol brings to the chairs

Fine line and botanical work is a style we have been asked for often and never quite had the right hand for. Sol does it properly, with the patience that kind of detail demands. It sits well alongside the bolder work the rest of the studio is known for.

  • Delicate fine line pieces, small and detailed.
  • Botanical work, from single stems to fuller designs.
  • The patient, close-up style that rewards a second look.

Consultations are free and never rushed. Come in, look through the portfolio on the wall, and talk through an idea even if you are months away from committing to it.

Booking with Sol

The early dates are filling, but there is still room, and Sol is happy to talk through an idea well before you are ready to book. Fine line work in particular benefits from a bit of planning around placement.

Drop in during the week to see the portfolio in person, or send through some references and we will set up a consultation. There is no pressure to commit, and Sol would rather get the idea right than rush you into the chair.
